known_vcs.notes: Very active in Scotland, particularly tech/software
Edinburgh-based, strong Scottish focus. Merged with Praetura Ventures in 2025 to form PXN Ventures — see PXN Ventures entry.
Formed from the merger of Par Equity and Praetura Ventures in 2025. Co-led the £2.5m seed round for Quantcore alongside Blackfinch and Scottish Enterprise.
Fund manager for the British Business Bank's Investment Fund for Scotland. Highly active across sectors; appeared in Highway Data Systems, Nami Surgical, and Esk deals in May 2026.
European deeptech and climate VC. Led the £10m seed round for Edinburgh's Exergy3 in April 2026 — largest Scottish deal that run.

Glasgow-based deeptech accelerator and investor operating from Skypark. Invested in Quantcore, Vuabl, and Airspection as part of a £772k cohort round in early 2026. Aspires to be UK's most active deeptech investor.
Led the $3m pre-seed round for Edinburgh's Cnuic in April 2026. HQ uncertain — co-founder Martin Drdúl may be Central European.
Led the £1.9m growth round for Stirling-based Nami Surgical in January 2026. Very limited public profile.
Bavarian state-backed VC. Participated in the £10m seed round for Edinburgh's Exergy3 in April 2026.
Quantum-focused VC fund. Participated in the £2.5m seed round for Glasgow's Quantcore.
Participated in the £1.58m seed round for Edinburgh's BIOCAPTIVA in March 2026. Limited public profile.
Quasi-public, co-invests with VCs
UK-based climate/cleantech VC. Co-invested in Exergy3 £10m seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026).

Singapore-based private investment office. Impact/ESG lean — tagline 'Investing with purpose'. Co-invested in Exergy3 £10m seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026) alongside Axeleo Capital and Bayern Kapital.

Commercial subsidiary of the British Business Bank. Makes direct and fund investments to support UK smaller businesses. Co-invested in BIOCAPTIVA £1.58m seed round (Edinburgh, March 2026) alongside Archangels.
University of Glasgow's investment arm. Backs Glasgow spinouts at pre-seed and seed stage. Co-invested in Entourage AI $5m pre-seed (March 2026).

Female-founded London impact VC, FCA-regulated. Runs the Conduit EIS Impact Fund (est. 2021). Focus on sustainable industry, integrated health, and financial resilience. UK-only. Connected to the Conduit Club (4,000+ member network). Invested in Bioliberty £10.5m Series A (March 2026).
London-based fund (Cayman Islands structure). Existing investor in Bioliberty £10.5m Series A (March 2026). Very limited public profile — likely a family office or single-LP vehicle.

London-based angel syndicate focused on green/climate investments. Co-led Carbogenics $3m seed round (Edinburgh, January 2026). Backs early-stage cleantech, carbon, and energy transition companies across the UK.
US-based climate/cleantech VC. Co-led Carbogenics $3m seed round (Edinburgh, January 2026). Focus on carbon removal and nature-based solutions.
Led Entourage AI $5m pre-seed (Glasgow, March 2026). Likely UK-based but maintains a very low public profile — no website or other deals found. Healthtech/longevity focus inferred.
Biopharmaceutical-focused investor. Led EnteroBiotix £19m growth round (April 2026). Very limited public profile — may be a single-LP vehicle or family office.
Scotland's government-backed development bank, established 2020 with a £2bn mandate. Co-invests alongside private capital at Series A and above. Broad sector mandate — net zero, place, wellbeing.
Has Edinburgh office, growth-stage focus
Active across UK, some Scottish portfolio
EIS/SEIS focused, some Scottish deals
Scotland's oldest and largest angel syndicate. Website: archangelsonline.com. Leveraged £41m of investment in Scottish scaleups in 2025. Strong focus on life sciences, medtech, and software.
University of Edinburgh's venture fund, operating as part of Edinburgh Innovations (the university's commercialisation arm). Focuses on Edinburgh spinouts across deep tech, life sciences, and energy.
Has MEIF and other funds active in Scotland
Glasgow-based, West of Scotland focus
Impact/social enterprise focus
Life sciences specialist
University spinout specialist, active with Scottish universities
Edinburgh-based early-stage tech
Northern England focus, occasionally overlaps
Deep tech, has backed some Scottish spinouts

Participated in Cnuic $3m pre-seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026). Very limited public profile. Source: https://tech.eu/2026/04/28/cnuic-secures-eur3m-pre-seed-to-unlock-next-generation-photonic-chip-production/

UK's only dedicated pre-seed investor in AI-first startups; typically invests £100k–£750k. Led TileBio £1.6m seed round (Glasgow, March 2026). British Business Investments committed £10m to the fund in February 2025. Source: https://glasgowcityofscienceandinnovation.com/glasgow-healthtech-spinout-raises-1-6m-to-transform-cancer-diagnosis-with-ai/

Venture fund and institute focused on AI, deep science, 5G, biotech, and fintech. Long-term investor in Edinburgh-based stampfree.ai — participated in every fundraising round including the May 2026 £1.03m raise. Source: https://www.digit.fyi/edinburgh-ai-ecommerce-firm-raises-over-1m-in-new-investment/

Silicon Valley-based VC. Co-led Cnuic $3m pre-seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026) alongside Tensor Ventures. Source: https://tech.eu/2026/04/28/cnuic-secures-eur3m-pre-seed-to-unlock-next-generation-photonic-chip-production/

UK-based deep-tech VC. Participated in Cnuic $3m pre-seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026). Source: https://tech.eu/2026/04/28/cnuic-secures-eur3m-pre-seed-to-unlock-next-generation-photonic-chip-production/

UK generalist VC founded 2014, ~64 investments via 1 fund, 3 exits. Participated in Edinburgh-based Infrawatch's pre-seed round (May 2026) — only confirmed Scottish deal identified to date. Source: https://theedinburghreporter.co.uk/2026/05/edinburgh-cyber-security-start-up-raises-millions-in-pre-seed-round/ ; firm profile: https://www.crunchbase.com/organization/portfolio-ventures

Major international VC. Co-led Wordsmith AI $70m Series B (Edinburgh, June 2026). One of Europe's most prominent growth-stage funds. Source: https://dailybusinessgroup.co.uk/2026/06/wordsmith-ai-raises-52-million-amid-rapid-growth/

Led the pre-seed round for Edinburgh-based Infrawatch, a cybersecurity platform company, announced May 2026. Principal at Outward VC named as Andi Kazeroonian. Very limited public profile. Source: https://theedinburghreporter.co.uk/2026/05/edinburgh-cyber-security-start-up-raises-millions-in-pre-seed-round/

UK-based VC. Participated in Edinburgh-based Infrawatch pre-seed round (May 2026). Investor Sam Stone quoted in press release. Source: https://theedinburghreporter.co.uk/2026/05/edinburgh-cyber-security-start-up-raises-millions-in-pre-seed-round/

Scottish Borders-based angel investment syndicate founded in 2004. Members invest personal capital matched by Scottish Enterprise. Led £506k HonuWorx, £430k Kaly and £620k Sisaltech seed investments announced May 2026. Has deployed £6.8m across Scottish early-stage businesses in ~2.5 years, levering £40m+ total investment. Source: https://www.scotsman.com/business/tricapital-scots-syndicate-leads-ps3m-plus-deals-and-unveils-key-hire-8640052

London-based growth VC focused on European tech scaleups. Led Wordsmith AI $70m Series B (Edinburgh, June 2026), one of the largest Scottish tech deals in recent years. Portfolio includes n8n, Nabla, Camunda, Wolt, WeTransfer, ContentSquare. Source: https://dailybusinessgroup.co.uk/2026/06/wordsmith-ai-raises-52-million-amid-rapid-growth/

Has a Scotland office. Existing investor that re-participated in Aveni £12m growth round (Edinburgh, June 2026). Invests from Scottish office per press release. Ben Leslie listed as Investment Director at Puma Growth Partners. Source: https://scottishfinancialreview.com/2026/06/04/aveni-edinburgh-ai-wealth-startup-raises-12m/

Edinburgh-based angel investment network. Led the £700k seed round for Edinburgh-based Lentitek (May 2026). Also co-invests with Scottish Enterprise. Source: https://angelcapital.scot/news/lentitek-raises-700k-for-cancer-treatment-technology/

Participated in the $11m Series A for Edinburgh-based Launchpad (October 2025, announced February 2026). Specialist robotics/deep tech VC. Source: https://www.pxnventures.co.uk/launchpad-welcomes-strategic-investor-pxn-ventures/

Scottish angel syndicate. Participated in Lentitek £700k seed round (Edinburgh, May 2026). Also participated in Aurora Avionics £500k seed (Edinburgh, April 2025). Source: https://angelcapital.scot/news/lentitek-raises-700k-for-cancer-treatment-technology/

Participated in Cnuic $3m pre-seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026). Very limited public profile. Source: https://tech.eu/2026/04/28/cnuic-secures-eur3m-pre-seed-to-unlock-next-generation-photonic-chip-production/

Participated in Cnuic $3m pre-seed round (Edinburgh, April 2026). Very limited public profile. Source: https://tech.eu/2026/04/28/cnuic-secures-eur3m-pre-seed-to-unlock-next-generation-photonic-chip-production/

Scottish informal investor operating in the angel syndicate space. Participated in Lentitek £700k seed round (Edinburgh, May 2026) alongside lead Equity Gap, Gabriel-IS, and Scottish Enterprise. Source: https://angelcapital.scot/news/lentitek-raises-700k-for-cancer-treatment-technology/

Primarily a Northern England investor (NPIF ecosystem). Co-invested in JET Connectivity £1.2m Pre-Series A (Scotland, May 2026) alongside lead Tricapital Angels — first confirmed Scottish deal. Source: https://angelcapital.scot/deals/tricapital-angels-leads-1-2m-investment-into-sovereign-dual-use-5g-solutions/

UK-wide SEIS/EIS fund manager with 400+ portfolio companies across B2B software, fintech, medtech, and green tech. Led the seed round for Regeno (Glasgow wind turbine spinout, March 2026). Source: https://www.strath.ac.uk/whystrathclyde/news/2026/strathclyde-supportedstart-upsecuresbackingtodeveloplower-costwindenergy/

Impact/cleantech-focused investor. Co-invested in Regeno seed round (Glasgow wind turbine spinout, March 2026) alongside SFC Capital, Scottish Enterprise, Gabriel-IS, and British Business Investments. Very limited public profile. Source: https://www.strath.ac.uk/whystrathclyde/news/2026/strathclyde-supportedstart-upsecuresbackingtodeveloplower-costwindenergy/
US-based gaming-industry specialist investor. Led the £10m growth round for Edinburgh payments fintech BR-DGE (June 2026), alongside continued backer Greyfriars.
Edinburgh-based investment management company (56 George Street). Long-term backer of BR-DGE, continuing its support in the June 2026 £10m growth round alongside new investor Bettor Capital.
